heading: Overview
text:
# Equipment Spec — AHU-12 (Air Handling Unit)
## Overview
AHU-12 is a roof-mounted air handling unit serving Plant Room B. Manufacturer: Carrier, model 39M. Installed 2019. It conditions supply air for floors 3 to 5.

heading: Key Specifications
text:
# Equipment Spec — AHU-12 (Air Handling Unit)
## Key Specifications
- Supply airflow: 8,000 CFM nominal.
- Cooling coil: 6-row chilled water coil, design entering water temperature 6 degrees Celsius.
- Supply fan motor: 7.5 kW, 400 V three-phase, belt driven.
- Filter bank: MERV 13 panel filters, replaced every 90 days.

heading: Torque Specifications
text:
# Equipment Spec — AHU-12 (Air Handling Unit)
## Torque Specifications
- Compressor mounting bolts: 45 newton metres.
- Fan bearing housing bolts: 30 newton metres.
- Coil header flange bolts: 25 newton metres.

heading: Normal Operating Ranges
text:
# Equipment Spec — AHU-12 (Air Handling Unit)
## Normal Operating Ranges
- Supply air temperature: 13 to 15 degrees Celsius in cooling mode.
- Chilled water differential pressure: 50 to 70 kilopascals.
- Filter pressure drop alarm threshold: 250 pascals.

heading: Overview
text:
# Equipment Spec — CH-02 (Water-Cooled Chiller)
## Overview
CH-02 is a water-cooled centrifugal chiller in the basement plant room. Manufacturer: York, model YK. Installed 2018. It is the lead chiller for the building cooling loop.

heading: Key Specifications
text:
# Equipment Spec — CH-02 (Water-Cooled Chiller)
## Key Specifications
- Cooling capacity: 500 tons nominal.
- Refrigerant: R-134a, charge 180 kilograms.
- Compressor: single-stage centrifugal, 315 kW.
- Condenser water flow: 1,500 gallons per minute.

heading: Normal Operating Ranges
text:
# Equipment Spec — CH-02 (Water-Cooled Chiller)
## Normal Operating Ranges
- Chilled water supply temperature: 6 to 7 degrees Celsius.
- Condenser water supply temperature: 29 to 32 degrees Celsius.
- Oil pressure: 200 to 250 kilopascals.
- Approach temperature: below 1.5 degrees Celsius.

heading: Safety Cutouts
text:
# Equipment Spec — CH-02 (Water-Cooled Chiller)
## Safety Cutouts
- High condenser pressure trip: 1,200 kilopascals.
- Low evaporator pressure trip: 250 kilopascals.
- Low oil pressure trip: 140 kilopascals.

heading: F-203 — Low Refrigerant Pressure
text:
# Fault Code Reference
## F-203 — Low Refrigerant Pressure
Indicates evaporator pressure below the safe threshold. Common causes: refrigerant leak, blocked expansion valve, or low charge. Action: isolate the unit, check for leaks with an electronic detector, and escalate if charge is below 90 percent. Severity is usually high.

heading: F-117 — High Condenser Pressure
text:
# Fault Code Reference
## F-117 — High Condenser Pressure
Indicates condenser pressure approaching the trip limit. Common causes: dirty condenser tubes, low condenser water flow, or air in the loop. Action: verify condenser water flow and clean tubes. Severity is medium to high.

heading: F-088 — Supply Fan Belt Slip
text:
# Fault Code Reference
## F-088 — Supply Fan Belt Slip
Indicates the supply fan is not reaching commanded speed. Common causes: worn or loose belt. Action: inspect and re-tension or replace the belt. Severity is low to medium.

heading: F-301 — Low Oil Pressure
text:
# Fault Code Reference
## F-301 — Low Oil Pressure
Indicates compressor oil pressure below the trip limit. Common causes: oil pump wear, clogged oil filter, or low oil level. Action: stop the compressor and inspect the oil system. Severity is critical.

heading: F-145 — Dirty Filter
text:
# Fault Code Reference
## F-145 — Dirty Filter
Indicates filter pressure drop above the alarm threshold. Action: replace the panel filters. Severity is low.

heading: Common Parts
text:
# Parts Catalog and Maintenance History
## Common Parts
- P/N 88-22A — Supply fan belt for AHU-12. Stocked in the main store.
- P/N 45-90C — MERV 13 panel filter, pack of four, for AHU-12.
- P/N 12-77R — Oil filter element for CH-02 compressor.
- P/N 33-50V — Chilled water expansion valve for AHU-12 cooling coil.
- P/N 70-10G — R-134a refrigerant cylinder, 12 kilograms.

heading: Maintenance History — AHU-12
text:
# Parts Catalog and Maintenance History
## Maintenance History — AHU-12
- 2026-02-10: Replaced panel filters, part P/N 45-90C. Filter pressure drop returned to normal.
- 2026-04-22: Re-tensioned supply fan belt after fault F-088. No parts replaced.
- 2026-05-30: Annual coil clean completed. Supply air temperature within range.

heading: Maintenance History — CH-02
text:
# Parts Catalog and Maintenance History
## Maintenance History — CH-02
- 2026-01-15: Quarterly inspection. Oil pressure 230 kilopascals, all readings normal.
- 2026-03-18: Replaced compressor oil filter, part P/N 12-77R, after fault F-301 warning.
- 2026-05-02: Condenser tubes cleaned after fault F-117. Condenser pressure returned to normal.

heading: Replacing the AHU-12 Supply Fan Belt
text:
# Maintenance Procedures
## Replacing the AHU-12 Supply Fan Belt
1. Lock out and tag out the supply fan motor.
2. Loosen the motor base adjustment bolts.
3. Slide the motor to release belt tension and remove the old belt.
4. Fit the new belt and adjust tension to 10 millimetres deflection at mid-span.
5. Tighten the fan bearing housing bolts to 30 newton metres.
6. Remove the lockout and run the fan to confirm normal speed.

heading: Quarterly Chiller CH-02 Inspection
text:
# Maintenance Procedures
## Quarterly Chiller CH-02 Inspection
1. Record chilled water supply and condenser water temperatures.
2. Check oil pressure is within 200 to 250 kilopascals.
3. Inspect for refrigerant leaks at the compressor and flanges.
4. Confirm approach temperature stays below 1.5 degrees Celsius.
5. Log readings and raise a work order for any value outside range.

heading: Cleaning Condenser Tubes
text:
# Maintenance Procedures
## Cleaning Condenser Tubes
1. Isolate the condenser water side and drain.
2. Brush each tube with a nylon brush and flush with clean water.
3. Inspect for scaling and pitting.
4. Refill, vent air, and confirm condenser pressure returns to normal range.

heading: Checking Refrigerant Charge
text:
# Maintenance Procedures
## Checking Refrigerant Charge
1. Connect gauges to the service ports.
2. Compare evaporator and condenser pressures against the spec ranges.
3. If pressures indicate undercharge, leak test before adding refrigerant.
4. Record the charge weight added on the work order.
